text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, easier to understand, and simpler to maintain. For example, a developer might rename unclear variable names, break a long function into smaller parts, or remove duplicate logic. These changes do not add new features, but they significantly improve readability. Over time, if code is not refactored, it accumulates technical debt—problems that slow down future development. Regularly refactoring helps teams work faster and reduce bugs in the long run.
